title: Nonlinear wear-phase features from recent slope and acceleration
summary: F1 tyre behavior is not purely linear with age; warm-up, thermal saturation, and late-stint drop-off create phase changes.
rationale: F1 tyre behavior is not purely linear with age; warm-up, thermal saturation, and late-stint drop-off create phase changes. Recent F1 tyre-energy and tyre-management work emphasizes nonlinear degradation and temperature-window effects, which your current features only approximate with ratios and thresholds.
implementation_hint: Within each driver-race stint, compute leakage-safe recent slopes over the last 2-4 laps for `LapTime_Delta`, `LapTime (s)`, and `Cumulative_Degradation`; then add acceleration/curvature terms, piecewise break indicators (e.g. slope turned positive after being flat), and interactions with `Compound`, `RaceProgress`, and wet/slick. Keep everything based only on prior laps within the same stint.
expected_effect: Should capture the ‘cliff’ before stops better than static `TyreLife` and cumulative wear features, improving ranking among cars with similar tyre ages.
risk: Noisy lap times from traffic or incidents can contaminate short-window slopes; robust clipping or median-based deltas may be needed.
